title: Depressurisation apparatus for a solid phase pneumatic transport system
abstract: A depressurisation apparatus (120) for a solid phase pneumatic system (100) comprises an inlet (121) to the system and an outlet (130). A particulate filter (127) is disposed between the inlet (121) and the outlet (130) and a volume flow rate controller (132) or restrictor is disposed between the outlet and the particulate filter. The actual pressurised volume flow rate through the depressurisation system (120) remains approximately constant during depressurisation, and the particulate filter (127) is able to operate at the pressure within the solid phase pneumatic system (100). This allows use of a smaller filter areas and apparatus and dispenses with the requirement for the use of a separate dust-tight vessel or associated dust containing conduits to depressurise the solid phase pneumatic system.
claims: Claims 1. A depressunsation apparatus for a solid phase pneumatic system, the depressunsation apparatus comprising an inlet for connection to the solid phase pneumatic system and an outlet; a particulate filter disposed between the inlet and the outlet; and an adjustable volume flow rate controller between the outlet and the particulate filter, wherein the adjustable volume flow rate controller is configured to maintain the volume flow rate to, or within a predetermined threshold range of, a target volume flow rate. 2. The depressunsation apparatus of claim 1 , comprising a pressure differential apparatus for measuring a pressure differential across the particulate filter, wherein the volume flow rate controller is configured to adjust the adjustable flow rate controller based on measurements received from the pressure differential apparatus. 3. The depressunsation apparatus of claim 1 or 2, wherein the volume flow rate controller is configured to adjust its flow area to maintain the volume flow rate to, or to within a predetermined threshold range of, the target volume flow rate. 4. The depressunsation apparatus according to claim 2 or 3, wherein in use: when the pressure differential across the particulate filter increases, the volume flow rate controller reduces its flow area; and/or when the differential pressure across the particulate filter decreases, the volume flow rate controller increases its flow area. 5. The depressunsation apparatus according to any one of claims 2 to 4, comprising a processing resource configured to regulate the adjustable volume flow rate controller. 6. The depressunsation apparatus of any preceding claim, comprising a housing having a housing inlet and a housing outlet and a flow pathway extending therebetween. 7. The depressunsation apparatus of claim 6, wherein the housing inlet is the inlet of the depressunsation system. 8. The depressunsation apparatus of claim 6 or 7, therein the particulate filter is disposed, optionally removably disposed in the flow pathway through the housing. 9. The depressunsation apparatus of any preceding claim, wherein the volume flow rate controller is disposed at or near the outlet of the depressunsation apparatus. 10. The depressunsation apparatus of any preceding claim, comprising a vent valve. 1 1. The depressunsation apparatus of claim 10, wherein the vent valve is positioned between the particulate filter and the outlet. 12. The depressunsation apparatus of claim 11 , wherein the vent valve is positioned between the volume flow rate controller and the particulate filter. 13. The depressunsation apparatus of claim 11 , wherein the vent valve functions as the adjustable volume flow rate controller. 14. The depressunsation apparatus of any preceding claim, wherein the particulate filter is a cartridge or bag filter. 15. The depressunsation apparatus of any preceding claim, wherein the particulate filter is structurally supported and configured to be cleaned using a back-flow of gas through the depressunsation apparatus. 16. The depressunsation apparatus of any preceding claim, wherein the outlet of the depressunsation apparatus communicates with a gas recycling system, or wherein the outlet vents to atmosphere. 17. A solid phase pneumatic system comprising a depressunsation apparatus according to any preceding claim. 18. The solid phase pneumatic system according to claim 17, wherein the solid phase pneumatic system operates as a dense phase or lean phase conveyor. 19. The solid phase pneumatic system according to claim 17 or 18, wherein the solid phase pneumatic system is a batch feeding system, in which particulate material is dispensed from a holding vessel in batches of a predetermined size. 20. The solid phase pneumatic system according to claim 19, the solid phase pneumatic system is a gravimetric feeder, in which batch sizes are determined by monitoring weight changes in a portion of the apparatus. 21 The solid phase pneumatic system according to claim 20, wherein the gravimetric feeder comprises a holding vessel, and the batch sizes are determined by monitoring weight change of the holding vessel. 22. A holding vessel for a solid phase pneumatic system, comprising a depressurisation apparatus according to any one of claims 1 to 16. 23. The holding vessel according to claim 22, comprising an access port or a material shut- off valve, which can be opened when the holding vessel is depressurised, to refill the holding vessel. 24. The holding vessel according to claim 22 or 23, wherein the depressurisation apparatus is connected to an upper part of the holding vessel. 25. The holding vessel according to any one of claims 22 to 24, wherein the depressurisation apparatus comprises a housing, and the housing is connected directly to the holding vessel. 26. The holding vessel according to any one of claims 22 to 25, further comprising a scale, for monitoring the weight of the holding vessel. 27. A method of depressurising a solid phase pneumatic system, or a part thereof, such as a holding vessel, the method comprising; providing a flow pathway out of the pneumatic system or holding vessel; flowing gas from the pneumatic system/holding vessel along the flow pathway, from an inlet to the flow pathway; (i) through a particulate filter (ii) through an adjustable volume flow rate controller; and (iii) through an outlet from the flow pathway; and adjusting the adjustable volume flow rate controller in order to maintain the volume flow rate to, or within a predetermined threshold range of, a target volume flow rate, whilst flowing gas along the flow pathway. 28. The method of claim 27, comprising detecting a pressure differential across the particulate filter and adjusting the volume flow rate controller based on the measured pressure differential. 29. The method of claim 27 or 28, comprising adjusting the flow area of the volume flow rate controller to maintain the volume flow rate to, or to within a predetermined threshold range of, the target volume flow rate The method of claim 29, comprising reducing the flow area when the pressure differential across the particulate filter increases. 31 The method of claim 29 or 30, comprising increasing the flow area when the pressure differential across the particulate filter decreases. 32. The method of any one of claims 27 to 31 , comprising reversing the flow along the flow pathway so as to clean the particulate filter. 33. The method of any one of claims 27 to 32, comprising pressurising the solid phase pneumatic system by reversing the flow along the flow pathway. 34. The method of any one of claims 27 to 31 , wherein the flow pathway is provided by opening a vent valve. 35. The method of claim 34, wherein gas from the pneumatic system/holding vessel flows through the vent valve after it is filtered by the particulate filter. 36. The method of any one of claims 27 to 35, wherein the inlet is at the pressure of the pneumatic system/holding vessel. 37. The method of any one of claims 27 to 36, comprising maintaining a generally constant actual pressurised volume flow rate before the flow rate controller, along at least a part of the flow pathway, as the pressure at the inlet drops from an initial pressure to a threshold pressure. 38. The method of any one of claims 27 to 37, comprising closing the vent valve, when the pressure at the inlet and at the outlet has equalised, or when the pressure in the solid phase pneumatic system has reached a predetermined value. 39. The method of any one of claims 27 to 38, wherein the flow pathway is defined, at least in part, by the depressurisation apparatus according to any one of claims 1 to 16.
